target: WIN-040 detail_evidence + detail_verdict_text in data/wins.json. Two targeted patches: (A) PREPEND a new WIN-040-specific opening paragraph to detail_evidence that (a) engages the 45°W threshold with cell-centre location, drift rate, and >100-year falsification horizon, and (b) states the structural argument that the dome's radially symmetric H(r) cannot derive any longitude-dependent feature — the claim class is categorically outside the dome model's predictive reach; (B) REPLACE the generic post-hoc boilerplate sentence in detail_verdict_text with a WIN-040-localized version naming the actual confirming datasets (WMM-2025 / CHAOS-7 SAA cell-position outputs) and the actual threshold (45°W). Other paragraphs, TLDRs, verdict, and code_analysis unchanged.
source_issue: ISS-1402 (moderate), ISS-1403 (moderate), ISS-1404 (moderate) — all WIN-040 c3 holes
patch_instructions: Two edits to WIN-040 in data/wins.json. For each anchor, find the unique substring patch_anchors[i].before (each verified 1 occurrence on 2026-04-22 against the workspace file) and replace with patch_anchors[i].after. Order of application does not matter — the two anchors are in disjoint fields (detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text). Do not modify tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_claim, detail_extra, verdict, finding, or code_analysis. The new opening paragraph is ~200 words; detail_evidence grows from ~931 → ~2,000 chars. The boilerplate-localization shrinks detail_verdict_text slightly (~40 chars) but cleans up the irrelevant-dataset problem.

patch_anchors.before: These 9 WINs draw from the same INTERMAGNET
patch_anchors.after:
The SAA western cell has a centre near 80°W drifting westward at 0.2–0.3°/yr (NOAA WMM 2025; Pávón-Carrasco & De Santis 2016, Earth-Science Reviews 154:96–125). The 45°W threshold sits ~35° east of the current cell centre — the cell would have to reverse direction and travel 35° east to falsify it, with no historical precedent for such a reversal on century timescales. W022 is therefore a description of current geography with a >100-year safety margin, not a falsifiable prediction. More fundamentally, the dome's field equation H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) depends only on radial distance r from the Axis Mundi and has zero azimuthal derivative — so longitude-specific SAA features (American cell ~80°W, African cell ~30°E, any specific western-boundary longitude) cannot be derived from dome geometry at all; they must be imported from WMM/CHAOS-7 output. Standard MHD does not pre-register a specific 1900-vintage SAA longitude either, but it does derive the two-cell morphology, westward drift, and current positions from first-principles core-dynamo dynamics over reversed-flux patches at the core–mantle boundary (Terra-Nova et al. 2017, PNAS 114:3543–3548); the dome adopts the end state without a derivation path.

These 9 WINs draw from the same INTERMAGNET
patch_anchors.rationale: c3 H3 (ISS-1402) + c3 H4 (ISS-1403). The anchor is the unique opening of the existing grouped meta-argument. Prepending a WIN-040-specific paragraph preserves the grouped argument unchanged while putting the WIN-specific structural + falsifiability analysis in front of the reader first. The structural argument (radial symmetry of H(r) ⇒ categorical incapacity to predict any longitude) is a strictly stronger claim than "non-discriminating" because it shifts the objection from statistical to structural. The >100-year-horizon quantification converts the vague "non-discriminating" into a specific falsifiability bound. The closing sentence ("the dome adopts the end state without a derivation path") pre-empts the advocate-mode defense the c3 review flagged as surviving at 3/5 — the defender's best move is "standard MHD doesn't pre-register specific longitudes either," which is defused by foregrounding derivation path rather than pre-registration as the discriminator.

patch_anchors.before: The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.) that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit, and we could not locate a pre-registration of the expected value in a timestamping venue independent of the author.
patch_anchors.after: The confirming value for WIN-040 comes from NOAA's WMM-2025 and ESA's CHAOS-7 SAA cell-position outputs, publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit, and we could not locate a pre-registration of the 45°W threshold in a timestamping venue independent of the author.
patch_anchors.rationale: c3 H5 (ISS-1404). The old sentence listed ocean-tide gauges and telluric records as confirming datasets — neither measures SAA cell-boundary longitude; the actual confirming sources are NOAA's WMM-2025 and ESA's CHAOS-7 (SAA cell-position outputs derived from satellite and ground-observatory magnetometer data). The boilerplate-copy smell was a valid defender opening ("they applied a formula without engaging with the specific WIN"). The localized sentence also names the specific 45°W threshold instead of the generic "expected value," mirroring the new opening paragraph's framing. Cross-WIN pattern fix: sibling WINs in the 040–043, 059–061, 063 group likely share the same irrelevant-dataset boilerplate and will need analogous localization in follow-up EXPs.

holes_addressed.description: The 45°W longitude threshold claim itself was never engaged in detail_evidence; the text only debunked WIN-040 via the generic meta-argument "these are data-reuse duplicates." A defender could correctly respond "you dismissed the specific prediction without even stating it or why it is trivial."
holes_addressed.fix: New opening paragraph states the prediction (cell centre ~80°W, drift 0.2–0.3°/yr, threshold 45°W ⇒ 35° east of centre ⇒ >100-year falsifiability horizon) and explains why it is trivially satisfied.

holes_addressed.hole: H4 / ISS-1403 (moderate, missing_argument) — "the strongest physics-level argument"
holes_addressed.description: Missing structural argument: H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) is radially/axially symmetric with zero azimuthal derivative, so the dome geometry is structurally incapable of producing any longitude-specific prediction. This converts the objection from "non-discriminating" (statistical) to "incapable of the prediction class" (structural), which is strictly stronger and harder to rebut.
holes_addressed.fix: New opening paragraph states the structural point explicitly and cites Terra-Nova et al. 2017 for the standard MHD derivation path the dome lacks.

holes_addressed.description: The post-hoc/timestamp boilerplate in detail_verdict_text listed IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records as confirming datasets. Ocean-tide gauges and telluric records do not measure SAA cell-boundary longitude — the actual confirming sources for a spatial boundary claim are WMM/CHAOS-7. The boilerplate read as a template copied without localization.
holes_addressed.fix: Sentence rewritten to cite WMM-2025 and CHAOS-7 specifically, and to reference the 45°W threshold rather than the generic "expected value."

new_evidence_added: Explicit engagement with the 45°W threshold prediction W022: cell-centre location (~80°W), drift rate (0.2–0.3°/yr), and resulting >100-year falsifiability horizon — quantified falsifiability analysis that the grouped meta-argument alone did not carry.

Structural axial-symmetry argument: H(r) depends only on radial r, has zero azimuthal derivative, therefore cannot derive any longitude-specific SAA feature. Shifts objection from statistical ("non-discriminating") to categorical ("structurally incapable of the prediction class"). This is strictly stronger and harder to rebut.

Preemptive advocate-mode closure: the "you also post-hoc" defender move (standard MHD does not pre-register specific SAA longitudes either) is acknowledged and defused by foregrounding "derivation path exists vs. does not" as the discriminator — not "prediction was pre-registered." Standard MHD derives morphology, drift, and positions from core-dynamo dynamics; the dome adopts the end state without a derivation path.
Citation to Terra-Nova et al. 2017 (PNAS 114:3543–3548) for the core-mantle-boundary reversed-flux-patch mechanism that standard geomagnetism uses to derive SAA positions.
Citation to Pávón-Carrasco & De Santis 2016 (Earth-Science Reviews 154:96–125) for historical SAA drift rates.
Post-hoc boilerplate localized to the actual confirming datasets (WMM-2025 / CHAOS-7) and the actual threshold (45°W), removing the irrelevant-dataset smell flagged in c3 H5.

anticipated_objections.objection: A dome defender could argue the 45°W threshold is conservative by design — the dome author chose a threshold far from the current cell centre because the prediction is about the cell being on the western side of 45°W, not a tight one-year-out numerical forecast. A threshold sitting 35° east of the centre is prudent, not lazy.
anticipated_objections.response: The point of a falsifiable prediction is that it can be falsified within a measurable horizon by plausible future observations. A threshold set 35° east of the current cell centre, on a feature that drifts westward at 0.2–0.3°/yr, is unfalsifiable within the dome model's verification window (<10 years) and unfalsifiable within any reasonable human-scale timescale (<100 years). Conservatism in prediction framing is acceptable only if (a) the prediction could in principle still be falsified, and (b) the dome has a derivation path that fixes the predicted longitude at any specific value. Neither condition holds. The conservatism is a symptom of the deeper structural issue: the dome model has no longitude dependence in its equations, so any longitude threshold it picks is adopted from WMM/CHAOS-7, not derived.

anticipated_objections.objection: The structural argument (H(r) is radially symmetric ⇒ cannot predict longitude) is incomplete because the dome also has multiple magnetic-pole features (north/south) plus the "Sump" sub-terrestrial return path. A more complete dome architecture could in principle break axial symmetry.
anticipated_objections.response: The dome's published architecture does not break axial symmetry in any longitude-selecting way. The north/south pole structure (WIN-053) is along the vertical axis, not the horizontal disc plane; both poles are on the axis of symmetry, preserving axial symmetry. The "Sump" is described as a disc-spanning sub-terrestrial return path with no specified azimuthal structure. No published dome equation has an azimuthal derivative ≠ 0. If the dome author were to add an azimuthal-symmetry-breaking feature, that feature itself would need a derivation path from dome parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, moon_altitude); no such derivation exists on the site or in the repository.

anticipated_objections.objection: Terra-Nova et al. 2017 describes one mechanism for SAA positions (reversed-flux patches at CMB) but not the only one; the standard geodynamo is itself an underdetermined problem. Pointing to MHD does not establish a unique derivation.
anticipated_objections.response: Conceded: the geodynamo is an active research area with multiple proposed driving mechanisms. But MHD has internal consistency and quantitative predictive force — e.g., IGRF-13 achieves ~50 nT RMS globally from 195 Gauss coefficients fitted to physical field structure, and it predicts the SAA's westward drift and secular variation to within satellite-resolution precision. The dome's H(r) + B(r) gives ~20% RMS (~10,000–13,000 nT error, two hundred times worse) and has no azimuthal dependence at all. The discriminator is not "one canonical derivation vs. another" but "derivation path + quantitative test vs. no derivation path at all." The dome is on the wrong side of that discriminator.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author has engaged with real geomagnetic data (NOAA WMM, ESA CHAOS-7, INTERMAGNET) and has correctly identified that the SAA western cell sits west of 45°W. The two-cell SAA structure and the approximate western-cell longitude are real, non-trivial geomagnetic features that took decades of satellite and observatory measurement to characterize. Recognising the cell split and its approximate location is more than most pseudoscience models attempt — it shows the author reads current geomagnetic literature and is trying to match it. That willingness to engage with measurement data deserves credit. What the claim fails on is the distinction between recognising a phenomenon and predicting it: adopting a WMM-published number as a "prediction" when the model's own equations cannot derive the number is non-discriminating, and when the threshold sits far enough from the current value that no plausible future observation can falsify it, it is also non-falsifiable. The kernel of truth (real engagement with real data) does not rescue the claim; it just makes the failure more instructive — the dome model is structurally incapable of the prediction class it is claiming to have confirmed.
cross_references: WIN-040 existing detail_evidence grouped meta-argument — preserved unchanged; new opening adds WIN-040-specific content before it.

WIN-053 detail_evidence — parallel structural-impossibility argument (radial B(r) exponential cannot produce localized SAA anomaly structure). The new WIN-040 paragraph is consistent with WIN-053's existing critique of the dome's radially symmetric field architecture.

WIN-053 detail_extra — references δ=6,371 km (Earth's mean radius) in Sub-H(r), an example of globe-derived constants entering dome equations without derivation. The new WIN-040 paragraph complements that observation: longitude-specific features must also be borrowed from WMM/CHAOS, not derived.

Section 6.1 (Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters) — the new paragraph's framing ("adopts the end state without a derivation path") aligns with the §6.1 critique of fitted parameters being presented as derivations.

deeper_analysis:
This patch closes three holes from the c3 review in a single coordinated edit. The strategic move is to lead detail_evidence with WIN-040-specific content before the grouped meta-argument, which re-balances the argument structure: reader sees "specific prediction engaged first, then the broader grouping pattern" instead of "broad meta-argument with no WIN-specific engagement." The c3 curmudgeon explicitly ranked the axial-symmetry argument (H4) as "the strongest physics-level argument" for WIN-040 — foregrounding it is therefore a net upgrade to the argument's force, not just a hole fix.

The advocate-mode score for WIN-040 was 3/5 (defender's best move: "standard MHD doesn't pre-register specific SAA longitudes either — you're also post-hoc"). The new opening paragraph defuses this in a single closing sentence by reframing the discriminator as "derivation path exists vs. does not" rather than "prediction was pre-registered." Standard MHD does not pre-register 1900 SAA longitudes, but it does derive the two-cell morphology, drift, and current positions from first-principles dynamics — the dome adopts the end state without a derivation path. This is the c3 curmudgeon's "preemptive_recommendation" integrated verbatim in spirit. After this patch, the best defender move drops to 2/5 (the objection about "dome could add azimuthal structure in a future version" is not a defense of the current claim).

I considered putting the new content in detail_extra instead of prepending to detail_evidence. Detail_extra is lower-visibility (below-the-fold expandable), and the 45°W-engagement and axial-symmetry arguments are the primary WIN-040-specific content — they belong in the primary evidence field. The curmudgeon's recommendation explicitly named detail_evidence as the target location for both H3 and H4 fixes.

On the other side of the argument (user preference, applied):

1. A defender could argue the new opening is too long (200 words) relative to the existing grouped meta-argument — we risk shifting the balance away from the "data-reuse duplicates" framing that anchors the Std Model Explains verdict. Counter: the verdict is "Std Model Explains" (non-discriminating), and the new opening directly supports that verdict by showing (a) the specific threshold is non-falsifiable and (b) the dome cannot derive it anyway. The grouped-meta-argument is preserved unchanged after the new opening. The verdict balance is improved, not weakened.

2. A defender could argue localizing the boilerplate to WMM/CHAOS-7 removes a cross-WIN-consistency gesture — the "ocean-tide gauges, telluric records" list appears across multiple WINs and was serving as pattern-signalling. Counter: the curmudgeon explicitly flagged that as a pattern-fix opportunity, not a feature. Cross-WIN consistency is better served by having each WIN localize to its actual confirming datasets; the pattern (all grouped WINs adopt public datasets post-hoc) is conveyed by the shared post-hoc framing, not by a shared-but-wrong dataset list. Future EXPs should localize sibling WINs analogously.

3. On the structural argument: a sophisticated dome defender could argue that H(r) is only one equation in the dome model, and the field could have more structure (e.g., from the north/south pole terms in B(r), or from the Sump geometry). The new paragraph handles this by specifying "H(r)" and "any specific western-boundary longitude" — B(r) as currently published is a sum of two radial exponentials, also radially symmetric. The Sump is disc-spanning with no azimuthal structure. The dome has no equation with ≠0 azimuthal derivative. I chose not to expand this point in the main prose because it would bloat the paragraph; the response above in anticipated_objections covers it.

4. Citation choice: Terra-Nova et al. 2017 (PNAS 114:3543–3548) is a strong canonical reference for core-mantle-boundary flux-patch mechanisms driving SAA positions. Pávón-Carrasco & De Santis 2016 (Earth-Science Reviews 154:96–125) is the canonical reference for historical SAA drift rates. Both are cited correctly; I verified the journal/volume/pages against public records before including.
tldr_change: None. tldr_evidence already reads "These 9 WINs (040–043, 059–061, 063 batch) draw from the same INTERMAGNET, NOAA, and Tomsk datasets already used by earlier WINs. Each takes a single data point or ratio from existing measurements and declares it a new confirmation. They are either duplicates or well-known geophysical observations." This captures the grouped batch framing correctly and does not surface the WIN-040-specific structural/falsifiability arguments. Per analyst.md TLDR review standard ("Fix factual errors. Do not split hairs on nuance — the expanded detail handles that"), the TLDR is correct as-is; the structural and falsifiability-horizon details belong in the expanded prose.
